newsSince: Material in-window M&A: on June 19, 2026 MDA agreed to acquire US smallsat builder Blue Canyon Technologies (from RTX/Raytheon) for US$620m all-cash, adding ~US$3.5B to pipeline and a US defense foothold; on June 2 MDA was selected by BAE for the U.S. SSC MEO Epoch 2 missile-warning constellation. These build on a strong Q1 2026 (revenue $464.1m, +32%; adj. EBITDA $90.6m; $3.7B backlog; FY26 guide $1.7-1.9B).

sources.label: MDA to acquire Blue Canyon Technologies ($620m)
MDA selected by BAE for SSC MEO Epoch 2

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent AI-node lens: space data/robotics/geointelligence are high-value AI inputs. The $620m Blue Canyon deal (US smallsat foothold, +$3.5B pipeline) plus the BAE/SSC Epoch 2 win are concrete, in-window strategic catalysts that deepen the defense/space/edge node. Move up vs peers on rank 34.
verdicts.bull: Q1 revenue +32%, $3.7B backlog, $40B pipeline; Blue Canyon adds US defense access and smallsat scale; ge/ robotics franchises feed AI/geointel demand under-covered by US investors.
verdicts.neutral: Backlog is converting fast but declining y/y; the AI-input angle is real yet indirect vs core space-systems revenue.
verdicts.bear: Acquisition integration/financing risk after +101% YTD; backlog down to $3.7B from $4.8B; valuation rich and dependent on program execution.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Blue Canyon close + US defense program wins; Telesat Lightspeed/Globalstar ramp converting backlog.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Integration/leverage risk and program timing; rich valuation after doubling YTD.
